name: Vilnius County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the standard outer field of Lithuanian county flags
the field of the Vilnius arms

description: A blue field with a red inner panel bearing the coat of arms of Vilnius County in the centre: Saint Christopher in silver carrying the golden Christ Child across a river.
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, Vilnius County's flag follows the uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue outer border surrounding the county's heraldic field, here red, charged with the historic Saint Christopher arms of the capital.

features.symbolism: blue outer border in the Lithuanian county pattern, with a red inner field bearing the county arms

features.role: coat of arms of Vilnius County
features.symbolism: Saint Christopher carrying the Christ Child across a river — the historic arms of the city of Vilnius, here serving for the surrounding county
